headline: Micron’s New Ford Deal Isn’t Enough to Escape the Gravity of the Memory Selloff
summary: Micron Technology  stock fell Tuesday after South Korea’s memory-chip makers spooked investors in U.S. competitors.  Micron’s drop came after a 6.9% fall for its South Korean peer  Samsung Electronics  which reported a 19-fold rise in operating profit for the June quarter.  Beyond a one-day move, though, there are further signs Micron is locking in higher prices for its memory chips well into the future.

Beyond a one-day move, though, there are further signs Micron is locking in higher prices for its memory chips well into the future.

headline: Ford Vs. Tesla: 2 American Icons With Upside, Which to Buy
summary: Ford (NYSE:F) and Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) just closed the books on Q1 2026. Ford leaned on trucks, fleet software, and a raised outlook. Tesla leaned on margin recovery, FSD subscriptions, and a roadmap stuffed with robots. Both grew revenue. Only one is priced like a growth story. Trucks Carry Ford. Margins Carry Tesla. headline: Up 14.81% in a Year, COWZ Proves You Don’t Need Apple
summary: The Pacer US Cash Cows 100 ETF (CBOE:COWZ) is having a solid 2026, up 6.4% year to date and 14.81% over the past year through July 6. Yet a fund built expressly to own America’s biggest cash machines holds zero shares of Apple, arguably the most famous cash generator on the planet. That contradiction is ... headline: Micron Just Locked in a Deal With Ford. Why the Stock Is Dropping.
summary: Micron Technology  stock fell Tuesday after South Korea’s memory-chip makers spooked investors in U.S. competitors.  Micron’s drop came after a 6.9% fall for its South Korean peer  Samsung Electronics  which reported a 19-fold rise in operating profit for the June quarter.  The reaction to Samsung’s preliminary earnings report is profit-taking after a run-up in the stock over the past 12 months.

headline: IAA MOBILITY 2027 Registration Now Open - Record-Breaking Demand - More Than Half of Floor Space Already Accounted For
summary: Registration for IAA MOBILITY 2027 is officially open. Interest from the international mobility sector hits record-breaking demand even before the regular registration period started. More than 100 companies have pre-registered as rebookers. As a result, over a year ahead of the event (scheduled for September 7–12, 2027), 53 percent of the total space used at IAA 2025 (both downtown and at the exhibition center) has already been reserved.

headline: Micron Just Locked in a Big Customer With Ford. Why the Stock Is Dropping.
summary: Micron Technology  was falling early Tuesday after stock moves in South Korea spooked investors in the memory-chip sector.  Micron shares were down 6.0% at $925.61 in premarket trading.  Fellow memory-chip company  SK Hynix  fell 6.1%, as it prepares to list American depositary receipts on the Nasdaq on Friday, raising $28 billion.

headline: Is Ford Motor (F) Fairly Valued After Its Micron Chip Supply Deal?
summary: Ford Motor (F) is back in focus after announcing a long term supply agreement with Micron Technology to secure memory and storage chips for its next generation vehicles, a key input for increasingly software centric cars. See our latest analysis for Ford Motor. At a share price of US$13.83, Ford Motor has seen a 20.05% 90 day share price return and a 25.21% 1 year total shareholder return, suggesting recent momentum after a softer 30 day period. If the Ford chip agreement has you thinking...
